posts.text: nanoGPT v2 is out. Added flash attention, gradient checkpointing, and a cleaner training loop. ~40% faster on A100.
posts.summary: nanoGPT v2 ships with flash attention and 40% training speed improvement on A100.
posts.whyItMatters: A widely-used educational LLM baseline just got significantly faster.

posts.text: Energy-based models and world models are the path to human-level AI. Autoregressive LLMs cannot plan.
posts.summary: LeCun argues energy-based world models, not LLMs, are the path to human-level AI.
posts.whyItMatters: LeCun continues to advocate alternative architectures to transformer scaling.

posts.text: We are going to continue to push the frontier. o3 is just the beginning of what reasoning models can do.
posts.summary: Altman teases continued advances in reasoning models beyond o3.
posts.whyItMatters: Signal from OpenAI leadership on near-term roadmap priorities.

posts.text: New study: students who used AI tutors outperformed control groups by 1.3 standard deviations.
posts.summary: Study shows AI tutors boost student performance by 1.3 SD.
posts.whyItMatters: Strong empirical evidence for AI in education.

posts.text: World Labs has raised 230M Series A to build spatial intelligence - AI that understands and generates 3D worlds.
posts.summary: World Labs raises 230M to build spatial intelligence AI for 3D world understanding.
posts.whyItMatters: Major funding for spatial AI signals the next frontier.

posts.text: Claude 3.5 Sonnet now supports computer use in public beta.
posts.summary: Claude 3.5 Sonnet computer-use enters public beta.
posts.whyItMatters: Computer use brings AI to real-world task automation.

posts.text:
Introducing GPT-4o with enhanced multimodal capabilities

GPT-4o now processes images, audio, and text in real time.
posts.summary: GPT-4o gets enhanced real-time multimodal processing.
posts.whyItMatters: Real-time multimodal processing broadens accessible AI use cases.

posts.text:
Claude's Character

We trained Claude to have genuine values: intellectual curiosity, warmth, and a commitment to honesty.
posts.summary: Anthropic publishes essay on Claude's designed character and values.
posts.whyItMatters: Anthropic's approach shapes industry norms around alignment.

posts.text:
Gemini 2.0 Flash: Our fastest model yet

3x faster inference than Gemini 1.5 Flash with comparable quality.
posts.summary: Gemini 2.0 Flash delivers 3x faster inference than its predecessor.
posts.whyItMatters: 3x speed improvement makes real-time AI dramatically more cost-effective.

posts.text:
Reasoning with LLMs: A Survey

This post surveys chain-of-thought, self-consistency, tree-of-thought, and process reward models.
posts.summary: Survey of LLM reasoning techniques: CoT, self-consistency, ToT, and PRMs.
posts.whyItMatters: Bird-eye view of reasoning techniques from a top OpenAI researcher.

posts.text:
SmolLM2: Powerful small language models for on-device AI

135M-1.7B models for device-constrained benchmarks. Apache 2.0.
posts.summary: Hugging Face releases SmolLM2 (135M-1.7B) for on-device AI.
posts.whyItMatters: Small open-source models democratize AI for mobile and edge deployments.

posts.text: The obsession with scaling transformers is a local optimum. JEPA is one promising direction away from it.
posts.summary: LeCun advocates for JEPA architecture as alternative to transformer scaling.
posts.whyItMatters: A leading researcher's architecture thesis challenging the scaling consensus.

posts.text: People who use AI consistently underestimate how much it improved their output. Our mental models of AI capability are lagging.
posts.summary: Research shows people underestimate AI contribution to their work quality.
posts.whyItMatters: Understanding AI impact is crucial for adoption decisions.

posts.text: The bitter lesson is still bitter. Simple algorithms + scale + compute beat clever algorithms + small scale. Every time.
posts.summary: Karpathy reaffirms the Bitter Lesson: simple algorithms at scale consistently win.
posts.whyItMatters: Reinforces the dominant paradigm driving current AI investment.

posts.text:
The Llama 3 Herd of Models

Authors: Dubey, Kumar, Mahajan, Ghosh et al.

We introduce Llama 3, trained on over 15 trillion tokens. Llama 3 70B matches GPT-4 on most benchmarks while remaining fully open-source.
posts.summary: [Paper] The Llama 3 Herd of Models

posts.text:
Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback

Authors: Bai, Jones, Ndousse, Askell et al.

We present Constitutional AI, training a harmless assistant using AI-generated feedback.
posts.summary: [Paper] Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback

posts.text:
Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models

Authors: Wei, Wang, Schuurmans, Bosma et al.

Generating a chain of thought significantly improves LLMs on arithmetic and symbolic reasoning.
posts.summary: [Paper] Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models

posts.text:
OpenAI releases o3 and o4-mini

https://openai.com/o3
posts.summary: OpenAI releases o3 and o4-mini

posts.text:
Show HN: Local-first AI coding assistant that runs entirely in-browser

https://github.com/example/local-ai-coder
posts.summary: Show HN: Local-first AI coding assistant runs entirely in-browser

posts.text:
Anthropic raises 4B from Amazon - Claude to power AWS Bedrock

https://www.anthropic.com/news/amazon-investment
posts.summary: Anthropic raises 4B from Amazon; Claude to power AWS Bedrock

posts.text: Ask HN: What is your workflow for using LLMs in production?
posts.summary: Ask HN: What is your workflow for using LLMs in production?
